seoTitle: How to Overlay Video in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to overlay video in Canva to layer clips, add depth, and create more dynamic promotional or social content.
intro: Overlaying video clips lets you combine multiple pieces of footage or add motion graphics on top of a base video, giving your content more visual depth. This is useful for marketers and content creators looking to make polished promotional material. This guide covers layering videos in Canva.

steps.heading: Add your base video
steps.body: Upload your main video clip and place it on your design's timeline as the background layer.

steps.heading: Add a second video or graphic on top
steps.body: Upload the overlay clip or graphic and drag it onto the canvas above your base video.

steps.heading: Resize and position the overlay
steps.body: Adjust the overlay's size and placement so it complements rather than overwhelms the base footage.

steps.heading: Adjust transparency for blending
steps.body: Lower the overlay's opacity if you want it to blend more subtly into the video beneath it.

steps.heading: Preview and export
steps.body: Play through the layered video to check the timing and blend, then export your finished project.

faq.question: Can I overlay more than two video clips at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can stack multiple video and graphic layers, though too many can make the timeline harder to manage.

faq.question: Does overlaying videos require Canva Pro?
faq.answer: Basic layering is available on the free plan, though some premium video assets and effects require Pro.

faq.question: Can I sync the overlay timing with the base video?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust each clip's start and end points on the timeline to align them precisely.

faq.question: Will overlaying reduce my video's quality?
faq.answer: No, Canva renders the final export at your chosen quality setting regardless of how many layers were used.
